tasks.title: Build generative art piece v1

tasks.title: Dashboard v2: per-project token cost + usage sparkline

tasks.acceptance: engine scripts and skills work on Windows (chrome.exe path, python vs python3); documented in CLAUDE.md env notes; verified by running server + a screenshot locally.

tasks.title: Dashboard: add-task form - queue a task from the UI, associate to an existing epic or create a new project epic
tasks.acceptance: Dashboard shows an Add Task form: title, size S/M, epic dropdown listing existing epics plus 'new project' (asks title+slug). server.py POST /api/task validates and writes it via board logic, placing it at a chosen queue position (default top). New task appears on the kanban within one refresh. Verified end-to-end in a real browser.
tasks.handoff: POST /api/task verified both paths (existing epic top-of-queue, new epic); form screenshot-verified in Chrome

tasks.acceptance: get a better estimation of the token budget. last time was false
tasks.handoff: DONE so far (this session): autopilot now reacts to the REAL Claude CLI usage-limit (lib.looks_like_limit/parse_reset_time), records it (lib.record_limit_hit: calibrates budget down to real spend + sets window.not_before), and naps to the exact reset then auto-resumes with no manual restart. REMAINING: switch per-run counting from the weighted-transcript heuristic to real usage/cost from 'claude -p --output-format json'; and resume-a-blocked-task-from-saved-state.

tasks.title: dashboard improvement. at the moment nothing is really linked, it just look like that but is fake links
tasks.acceptance: ### Phase 1: Task Initialization * **Trigger:** A new task is assigned to you on the Dashboard. * **Action:**    1. Read the task details (title, description, initial tags).   2. Open a corresponding **GitHub Issue** in the repository.   3. Map the Dashboard tags to the appropriate **GitHub Labels (tags)** and apply them to the GitHub issue.   4. Update the Dashboard task with a link to the newly created GitHub Issue so they are linked.  ### Phase 2: Tag Synchronization * **Trigger:** A tag/label is added or removed on either the Dashboard or GitHub. * **Action:** Instantly sync the change. If a tag is added on GitHub, update the Dashboard task with that tag, and vice versa.  ### Phase 3: Task Completion & Merging * **Trigger:** You finish the work and are ready to close the task. * **Action:**   1. Create a **Pull Request / Merge Request (PR/MR)** for your branch.   2. Link the PR/MR to the GitHub Issue using close keywords (e.g., "Closes #IssueNumber").   3. Once the PR/MR is approved and successfully merged, ensure the GitHub Issue is closed.  ### Phase 4: Final Dashboard Update * **Trigger:** The GitHub Issue is closed and the PR/MR is merged. * **Action:**   1. Mark the Dashboard task as "Completed" / "Done".   2. Retrieve the merged **MR/PR Link** from GitHub.   3. Write/attach this MR/PR Link directly into the finished Dashboard task's designated field (e.g., description, comments, or custom link field).
tasks.handoff: Shipped real GitHub traceability. lib.commits_by_task maps each task to its shipping commit sha via git log, build_dashboard enriches tasks with commit, dashboard renders real commit-URL links on cards and detail modal. Fixes the fake-links complaint, verified in Chromium. Full 4-phase issue/PR/tag automation needs gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN not present here, filed as T27.

tasks.title: GitHub API automation: auto-create issues + PR close-keywords (needs gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN)
tasks.acceptance: When gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN is available: board.py claim opens a real GitHub issue and stores its number; board.py done opens/links a PR with 'Closes #N'; dashboard tag edits sync to GitHub labels both ways. Blocked in the current headless local env which has neither gh nor a token — the commit-link traceability from T26 is the token-free fallback.

queue.note: Order of `next` is priority. board.py keeps this in sync with backlog.json statuses.

sessions.did: Bootstrap: constitution, state system, engine scripts (track/budget/board/dashboard/server/drive), 5 skills, realtime dashboard, CI cron, and 3 shipped projects (phone teardown, NEON DRIFT game, Silk Fields art). T1-T4, T6, T7, T9, T10 done.
sessions.resume_with: python3 engine/board.py brief — T11 (journal.py) is top of queue

sessions.did: Migration: session moved from cloud container to local Windows machine; cloud commits were unreachable, so all files were restored from conversation context and pushed to github.com/metasito/creativeworld. Filed T12 (cross-platform support).
sessions.resume_with: python engine/board.py brief — T11 top of queue; note Windows env (python, chrome.exe)

sessions.did: Work session: T11 journal.py, then T13 dashboard add-task form (user prio 1)

learnings: 2026-07-14: Cloud-container work is unreachable once a session moves machines — push to a remote EARLY; a repo without a remote is one container-reclaim away from losing everything.

2026-07-14: Virtual-time screenshots lie for rAF pages — use `engine/drive.py` (real-time WebDriver; needs chromedriver `--disable-build-check` with the bundled Chromium, and `--no-sandbox --disable-gpu` everywhere).

2026-07-14: URL test hooks are gold: `?p=` (pin scroll progress), `?demo=1` (autopilot + state mirrored into document.title), `?seed=` (deterministic art) make every project verifiable headlessly for pennies.
2026-07-14: Cost calibration — full bootstrap (engine + dashboard + 3 shipped projects) ≈ 1.23M weighted tokens; one M-sized single-file project ≈ 150–250k; an S task ≈ 40–80k.

2026-07-14 (bootstrap): Transcript JSONL at `~/.claude/projects/<proj>/<session>.jsonl` carries per-turn `usage`; weighted formula in tokens.json config approximates plan units — calibrate, don't trust.

log.msg: T24 recovery: verifying GitHub traceability before committing prior session's work
T26: wired tasks to real commit URLs; verifying in Chromium
T26 done: real commit-link traceability shipped; T27 filed for gh-API automation
T18: tint+touch coded, verifying in Chromium
T20: presets + trail slider + mobile CSS built; verifying in Chromium
T20 verified: binary + solar presets orbit stably, mobile 390px layout OK
